entities.name: EVAPOTRANSPIRATION
entities.description: The combined process of water evaporation from soil and transpiration from plants.

entities.name: EVAPORATION
entities.description: The process of water turning into vapor from surfaces.

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The measure of heat in the atmosphere.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: Regions characterized by low rainfall and high temperatures.

entities.name: AGRICULTURE
entities.description: The practice of farming and crop production.

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Long-term shifts in global or regional weather patterns.

entities.name: REFERENCE CROP EVAPOTRANSPIRATION
entities.description: A standardized measure of evapotranspiration used as a baseline for crop water needs.

entities.name: PATANCHERU
entities.description: A specific study site in Andhra Pradesh, India.

entities.name: ANDHRA PRADESH
entities.description: A state in India where the study was conducted.

entities.name: ENERGY BALANCE TERM
entities.description: A component of evapotranspiration related to the energy available for water phase change.

entities.name: AERODYNAMIC TERM
entities.description: A component of evapotranspiration related to the transport of water vapor away from the surface.

entities.name: KHARIF
entities.description: The monsoon crop season in India, typically from June to October.

entities.name: RABI
entities.description: The winter crop season in India, typically from November to February.

entities.name: SOLAR RADIATION
entities.description: The electromagnetic radiation emitted by the sun.

entities.name: CROP MODELLING
entities.description: The use of mathematical simulations to predict crop growth and water use.

entities.name: REGIONAL CIRCULATION MODELS
entities.description: Climate models that simulate atmospheric and oceanic processes at a regional scale.

entities.name: GLOBAL CIRCULATION MODELS
entities.description: Climate models that simulate atmospheric and oceanic processes at a global scale.
